title: Sweet Tender Cornmeal Pilaf
description: A sweet and tender Argentinian dish featuring fine yellow cornmeal, steamed to perfection for a show-off side dish or appetizer that's kosher-friendly.

ingredients.name: 1 cup finely ground yellow cornmeal
ingredients.notes: Ensure it's fine and consistent for even cooking

ingredients.name: 2 cups water or vegetable broth
ingredients.notes: Use warm for easier incorporation into the cornmeal mix

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: Gently melts in the mixture for added richness

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon lemon juice or apple cider vinegar

instructions: Combine cornmeal with water and salt (if using) in a saucepan. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Gradually stir in sugar until dissolved. If the mixture starts to scorch at any point, turn off heat and add another splash of liquid to prevent burning.
Remove from heat, fold in butter gently and mix well. Stir in lemon juice or vinegar (if using) for extra brightness.
Pour into a greased baking dish or shallow pan lined with parchment paper for easy removal. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for about 20-25 minutes, until golden brown and lightly crispy around the edges.
